research_method: OpenAI Responses API (model: gpt-5.6-luna) with the web_search tool, single research call requesting exactly 3 candidates scored on 6 fixed criteria.

candidates.description: Apple sued OpenAI and two former Apple employees on July 10, 2026, alleging that confidential Apple information was taken to help OpenAI develop consumer hardware.
candidates.primary_source_note: U.S. federal court docket and Apple's filed complaint are primary legal documents; the allegations themselves remain unproven.
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The legal conflict: what Apple alleges OpenAI and former employees did. 2) The business conflict: why OpenAI's planned consumer hardware makes the dispute significant for Apple.

score_notes.clear_hook: A familiar Apple-versus-OpenAI rivalry has become a courtroom fight over alleged stolen secrets.
score_notes.two_point_fit: Clearly separates into legal allegations and the broader battle over future AI hardware.
score_notes.general_user_impact: Listeners are not directly affected yet, but the case could influence future devices, assistants, and privacy practices.
score_notes.audio_only_comprehensibility: Explainable without screens: one company says the other used confidential information to build competing products.
score_notes.source_reliability: Confirmed by a court filing and multiple major news organizations.
score_notes.fact_stability: The filing and parties are stable facts, but the allegations and eventual legal outcome may change.

candidates.description: Meta removed an Instagram-related AI image feature ("Muse Image") within days of launching it, after users and the performers' union SAG-AFTRA objected to public photos being usable as references without clear consent.
candidates.primary_source_note: Meta's own official product announcement and its July 10, 2026 update are primary-source information.
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The product decision: what the AI feature did and why Meta launched it. 2) The consent question: whether publicly posted images should automatically be available for AI image generation.

score_notes.clear_hook: A major technology company launched an AI feature and reversed it almost immediately after public criticism.
score_notes.two_point_fit: Naturally supports a product-design discussion and a separate privacy-and-consent discussion.
score_notes.general_user_impact: Instagram users may care directly about how their public photos and likenesses can be used.
score_notes.audio_only_comprehensibility: Explainable with a simple example: someone uses a public Instagram photo as a reference for a new AI image.
score_notes.source_reliability: Meta, Reuters, AP, and TechCrunch independently confirm the central event.
score_notes.fact_stability: The launch, criticism, and removal are completed events; only longer-term policy implications remain uncertain.

candidates.description: ASML raised its 2026 sales forecast and announced major production-capacity expansion after reporting stronger-than-expected second-quarter results driven by demand for equipment used to make AI chips.
candidates.primary_source_note: ASML's official second-quarter results and financial outlook are primary-source company information.
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The business result: ASML's unusually strong earnings and higher forecast. 2) The technology-chain implication: AI demand increasing pressure on the factories and machines needed to make chips.

score_notes.clear_hook: Demand for AI chips is strong enough to make a major chip-equipment supplier expand production.
score_notes.two_point_fit: Divides naturally into ASML's financial performance and the wider physical infrastructure behind AI.
score_notes.general_user_impact: Effects are indirect, but chip supply can influence price, availability, and capability of phones, computers, and AI services.
score_notes.audio_only_comprehensibility: Works verbally if ASML is described simply as a company that makes machines for chip factories; some technical terms need care.
score_notes.source_reliability: Core numbers come from ASML's official results and are independently reported by Reuters and other reputable outlets.
score_notes.fact_stability: Earnings, forecast, and announced expansion are completed, documented events.
